name: Webroot
tagline: Ultra-lightweight cloud-based antivirus with tiny footprint
description: Webroot SecureAnywhere takes a unique cloud-based approach, offering a tiny 2MB installation that uses almost no local system resources.
longDescription: Webroot takes a fundamentally different approach to antivirus. Instead of downloading massive signature databases to your device, Webroot maintains everything in the cloud. The local client is a tiny 2MB installation that performs scans in seconds rather than hours. When it encounters an unknown file, it analyzes it in the cloud in real-time. This approach has clear advantages: there is almost no storage footprint, scans are blazingly fast, and updates happen instantly without downloading anything. The downside is that you need an internet connection for full protection.

keyFeatures.name: Cloud-Based Scanning
keyFeatures.description: All threat analysis performed in the cloud using real-time intelligence. Local client is just 2MB. Scans complete in 30 seconds.

keyFeatures.name: Identity Shield
keyFeatures.description: Monitors and blocks unauthorized access to usernames, passwords, credit card numbers, and other sensitive data. Prevents keyloggers.

keyFeatures.name: Journaling & Rollback
keyFeatures.description: Records all system changes and can reverse them if malware is detected. Restores encrypted or damaged files to their pre-attack state.

keyFeatures.name: Real-Time Anti-Phishing
keyFeatures.description: Cloud-based URL reputation analysis that blocks phishing sites. Updated continuously as new threats emerge.

verdict: Webroot is the ideal choice for users with older or low-resource computers who need protection without performance impact. The 2MB installation and 30-second scans are genuinely impressive. However, the below-average detection rates mean it is not suitable as a primary antivirus for users handling sensitive data.

faqs.question: Can Webroot work offline?
faqs.answer: Webroot performs best with an active internet connection, as its threat analysis engine is cloud-based. However, it does include a basic set of offline definitions for known major threats.

faqs.question: How fast are Webroot scans really?
faqs.answer: In our testing, Webroot's full system scan completed in an average of 28 seconds, compared to 5-15 minutes for traditional antivirus products. It is genuinely the fastest scanner available.

author.name: Sarah Mitchell
author.title: Senior Tech Reviewer
author.bio: Sarah has been evaluating cybersecurity products for 7 years.
